title: penstemon glaber var. brandegeei
intro: Penstemon glaber var. brandegeei, also known as Brandegee's beardtongue, is a perennial plant species belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and relieving respiratory problems. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Brandegee's beardtongue have been made from the plant's roots and leaves, which are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures.
